title: automate dpi header inclusion only for the files that need it
body:
As mentioned in the [verilator documentation](https://verilator.org/guide/latest/connecting.html#direct-programming-interface-dpi:~:text=%22svdpi.h%22-,%23include%20%22Vour__Dpi.h%22,-int%20add()), verilator generates a dpi header based on verilog/systemVerilog dpi imports, and we should include that dpi header in the c/c++ files that we define our dpi functions. 

if I were to use `dpi_defns.c` with different `top.sv` files or with different `--prefix`es, verilator would generate dpi headers with different names right? then should we write a separate copy of the `dpi_defns.c` to include each different dpi header with same content but different name? Or even more, I want to send my `dpi_defns.c` file to another person, and I don't want to bother him with modifying the `#include "<prefix>__Dpi.h"` line. 

and I don't want to `--compiler-include <prefix>__Dpi.h` for all my c/c++ files, since there may be hundreds of c++ files (I don't know if `--compiler-include` affects the preprocessing of verilator generated files, most of the c++ files comes from that pathway) and this will increase preprocessing time. 


is it possible to include generated dpi header file only in the files that need it?
